questions.title: Packaging waste
questions.question: Customers complain about packaging waste. What should your company do?
questions.info: Packaging impact depends on both material type and total waste volume. Plastic-heavy packaging tends to create higher end-of-life impact, recyclable materials improve recovery, and minimal packaging avoids material use at the source.
questions.best_reason: Minimal packaging is best because avoiding material use usually beats only changing the material.

questions.title: Goods transport
questions.question: You need to organize how goods are transported between warehouses.
questions.info: Freight emissions vary strongly by mode. Rail generally emits much less per tonne-kilometre than road freight, while trucks are more flexible but more carbon-intensive.
questions.best_reason: Using trains when possible gives the strongest environmental improvement.

questions.title: Employee work policy
questions.question: You need to define the company work policy.
questions.info: Work policy affects commuting and office energy. Remote work can reduce commuting emissions, but home-energy rebound effects should still be considered.
questions.best_reason: Fully remote work scores best when commuting and office-space use are actually reduced.

questions.title: Energy supplier
questions.question: Your company must choose an energy supplier.
questions.info: Energy supplier choice changes electricity carbon intensity. Renewable electricity is the strongest option, mixed energy is neutral, and cheap unspecified energy may rely on higher-carbon sources.
questions.best_reason: Renewable energy has the best environmental score.

questions.title: Cybersecurity investment
questions.question: You need to decide how much to invest in cybersecurity.
questions.info: Cybersecurity has a small operational footprint, but weak security can create incidents with financial and operational damage. Advanced security adds some compute/storage overhead in this model.
questions.best_reason: Minimal and standard security are tied environmentally; A is marked best only by environmental score, though it increases cyberattack risk.

questions.title: Cyberattack response
questions.question: Your company is hit by a cyberattack that disrupts operations.
questions.info: Incident response can require extra recovery activity and infrastructure use. Slow restoration has little direct environmental effect but can hurt the business financially.
questions.best_reason: Slow restoration has the best direct environmental score, although it may not be the best business decision.

questions.title: Supplier choice
questions.question: A supplier is very cheap but not environmentally friendly.

choices.text: Work with the supplier to improve

questions.info: Supplier choice affects upstream production footprint. A cheap but polluting supplier shifts environmental impact into the supply chain; sustainable procurement reduces that footprint.
questions.best_reason: Choosing the sustainable supplier gives the highest environmental score.

questions.title: New office location
questions.question: You need to open a new office for a growing team.

choices.text: Open the office in the city center, with easy access but higher rent
Open the office in a suburban area, cheaper but requiring more commuting

questions.info: Office location affects commuting. City-center locations often support public transport and shorter trips, while suburban locations can increase car dependence.
questions.best_reason: The city-center office is better because it supports lower-carbon commuting.

questions.title: Marketing campaign
questions.question: The marketing team wants to launch a promotional campaign.

choices.text: Use a limited eco-designed print campaign combined with digital communication

questions.info: Marketing campaigns have material and distribution impacts. Mass printed flyers create paper, ink, and logistics footprint; digital communication avoids most physical material use.
questions.best_reason: A limited eco-designed print campaign combined with digital communication gives the best score.

questions.title: Printing policy
questions.question: Employees print too many documents.

choices.text: Set double-sided black-and-white printing by default
Require digital documents for internal meetings

questions.info: Printing uses paper, ink, hardware, and energy. Duplex black-and-white printing reduces material use, while digital internal documents avoid most printing entirely.
questions.best_reason: Digital documents for internal meetings best avoid printing impact.

questions.title: Data backup strategy
questions.question: You need to choose a data backup strategy.
questions.info: Backup strategy balances resilience with storage and transfer footprint. Continuous backups duplicate more data, while weekly backups reduce footprint but increase data-loss risk.
questions.best_reason: Weekly backups use the least backup storage and transfer in the environmental model.

questions.title: Cycling support
questions.question: Employees ask for better cycling support.

choices.text: Do nothing because few employees currently cycle

questions.info: Cycling support can shift commutes toward near-zero direct-emission transport. Doing nothing preserves existing commuting patterns.
questions.best_reason: A bike bonus can produce a strong positive commuting modal shift.

questions.title: Product returns
questions.question: Customers are returning more products, increasing transport and waste.

choices.text: Improve product quality to reduce returns

questions.info: Product returns create reverse logistics, extra handling, and sometimes waste. Preventing returns at the source through better quality is usually stronger than only discouraging returns later.
questions.best_reason: Improving product quality prevents returns at the source and has the highest environmental score.

questions.title: Office cooling and heating
questions.question: Your office energy consumption is rising. How should you improve cooling and heating?
questions.info: Office cooling and heating depend on building demand and active energy use. Insulation reduces demand, while smart controls optimize usage over time.
questions.best_reason: Smart climate control systems score best in this model.

questions.title: Product replacement frequency
questions.question: Your products are replaced frequently, generating waste and increasing production costs. What should your company do?

choices.text: Offer a repair or refurbishment service

questions.info: Frequent product replacement creates waste and manufacturing impact. Longer life, repairable design, and repair/refurbishment reduce demand for new production.
questions.best_reason: Designing products for repair ties environmentally with repair services, but avoids extra service cost in this game.

outcomes.text: A cyberattack targets your company, but your security systems detect it early because you invested in good cybersecurity. The attack is stopped before it causes serious damage. Score change: Environment 0, Money 0.

A cyberattack hits your company and disrupts several systems before being stopped. No major data is lost, but recovery work increases energy use and slows operations, causing a financial loss. Score change: Environment -5, Money -10.

A cyberattack spreads across your systems without being detected in time because you did not invest enough in cybersecurity. Recovery requires extra IT work, system rebuilding, and emergency operations, causing major financial damage. Score change: Environment -10, Money -25.
Customers raise concerns about packaging waste, but since you already use minimal packaging, your company is praised for its efforts and avoids negative impact. Score change: Environment 0, Money 0.

Customers complain about packaging waste. While you switched to recyclable materials, some issues remain visible, so the company faces moderate criticism and loses some revenue. Score change: Environment 0, Money -8.

Customers strongly criticize your packaging choices, especially because of heavy plastic use. Complaints spread, customer satisfaction drops, and the extra packaging waste creates an additional environmental penalty. Score change: Environment -5, Money -18.

A journalist investigation reviews your company’s environmental practices. Because you invested in sustainable solutions and made responsible choices, the report highlights your efforts and trust is maintained. Score change: Environment 0, Money 0.

A media report exposes some environmental weaknesses in your operations. You followed some sustainable practices, but remaining issues attract criticism and slightly damage the company’s reputation. Score change: Environment 0, Money -12.

A major media scandal breaks out after your company is exposed for poor environmental practices, such as cheap suppliers, high-emission choices, or wasteful product decisions. Public trust drops sharply and the company suffers both reputational and environmental penalties. Score change: Environment -5, Money -25.

An internal error or technical failure leads to the loss of important company data. Your continuous backup systems allow fast and complete restoration, so the incident has no lasting consequences. Score change: Environment 0, Money 0.

An internal error or technical failure leads to the loss of important company data. Only part of the data can be recovered, causing minor disruption and extra work, but no direct environmental penalty. Score change: Environment 0, Money -10.

An internal error or technical failure leads to the loss of important company data. Due to weak backup practices, critical data is lost permanently, forcing duplicate work, recovery operations, and significant financial losses. Score change: Environment -5, Money -25.
